title: AEM API Forbidden Error
symptom: When attempting to create a new AEM page, the workflow returns a 'Forbidden' error, indicating potential credential issues.
applies_when: Using AEM API for page creation
CSRF token is not included in the request
root_cause: The error occurs because the AEM API requires a valid CSRF token for POST requests to prevent cross-site request forgery. If the token is missing or incorrect, the API will respond with a 'Forbidden' error.
fix_steps: Make a GET request to your AEM instance to retrieve the CSRF token from the response headers.
Include the retrieved CSRF token in the header of your subsequent POST request when creating the AEM page.
agent_instruction: Retrieve the CSRF token from AEM and include it in your page creation request.
